name: inventory_getswitches
description: Lists all switches in inventory with chassis_name, management ip_address, fabric, firmware and health (optionally filtered by fabric-id or id). Use this to resolve a switch NAME (e.g. 'Spine-1') to its management IP before calling per-device tools (BGP, interfaces, running-config, etc.).

name: inventory_switch_inventory_summary
description: Returns summarized inventory information for switches (identity, role, model, serial, IP, health).

name: fabric_get_fabrics
description: Get all fabrics with configuration, status, and device summary

name: fabric_get_fabrics_health
description: Get health status of all fabrics

name: fabric_get_fabrics_errors
description: Get all fabrics errors configured in the application

name: fabric_get_devices
description: Get all devices in the specified fabric (by fabric name)

name: fabric_get_physical_topology
description: Get fabric physical topology for a specified fabric

fabric_name.description: Fabric whose PHYSICAL topology (real per-port switch↔switch links) to fetch. Snake `fabric_name` or hyphenated `fabric-name` both accepted.

site.description: Site id when a fabric name exists on multiple sites (e.g. lab-a vs lab-b).

name: fabric_get_underlay_topology
description: Get fabric underlay topology for a specified fabric

fabric_name.description: Fabric whose UNDERLAY topology to fetch. Snake `fabric_name` or hyphenated `fabric-name` both accepted.

site.description: Site id when a fabric name exists on multiple sites (e.g. lab-a vs lab-b).

name: fabric_get_overlay_topology
description: Get fabric overlay topology for a specified fabric

fabric_name.description: Fabric whose OVERLAY topology to fetch. Snake `fabric_name` or hyphenated `fabric-name` both accepted.

site.description: Site id when a fabric name exists on multiple sites (e.g. lab-a vs lab-b).

name: fabric_validate_physical_topology
description: Validate fabric physical topology for a specified fabric

name: fabric_get_running_config
description: Get the running configuration CLI commands for the fabric

name: fabric_get_execution_list
description: Get the list of all previous fabric executions

limit.description: Limit the number of executions returned

status.description: Filter executions by status (failed | succeeded | all)

name: inventory_get_device_inventory_export
description: This API will export the device inventory information as a binary Excel file

name: auth_validate_token
description: Validate if the current authentication token is valid

name: auth_get_token_expiry
description: Get token expiry time for a given token type

name: auth_get_all_token_expiry
description: Get expiry times for all tokens

name: auth_get_client_by_name
description: Fetch API client details by name

name: auth_get_execution
description: Get detailed execution output by execution ID

name: auth_get_executions
description: Get the list of all previous executions, optionally filtered by status

limit.description: Limit the number of executions returned

status.description: Filter executions by status (failed | succeeded | all)

name: auth_get_exec_logs
description: Get Execution log list with pagination

name: auth_get_tacacs_by_host
description: Fetch TACACS  server details of a sepcific host or all hosts

name: auth_get_active_user_by_type
description: Get the active users details by authentication type

name: auth_get_user_by_name
description: Fetch all users details or specific user detail by name

name: auth_get_active_host_users
description: Get the host users and the active session details

name: system_get_health_status
description: Get overall system health and node status

name: system_get_supportsave_list
description: Get list of available SupportSave files

name: system_get_supportsave_status
description: Get the execution status of a SupportSave request using its request ID

id.description: Request ID returned when a SupportSave operation is initiated

policy.notes: Requires a request ID generated by the SupportSave creation API. Cannot be used with filenames returned by supportsave list.

usage.notes: The request ID is only available from the SupportSave creation response. Completed supportsave files do not expose execution IDs.

name: system_get_executions
description: Get the list of all the requests previously executed

limit.description: Limit the number of executions returned

status.description: Filter executions by status (failed | succeeded | all)

name: system_get_execution
description: Get the detailed output of a request previously executed, based on a given request ID

name: system_get_last_execution_diagnostic
description: Show the most recent failed system execution and why it failed

name: system_get_file_download
description: Get any file from XCO system.

name: monitor_get_service_status
description: Get monitoring status of all services

name: monitor_get_all_status
description: Get complete monitoring status (services, nodes, pods, resources)

name: monitor_get_certificate_expiry
description: Get expiry date of EFA certificates

name: notification_get_subscriber
description: Get a notification subscriber by ID

limit.description: Limit the number of executions returned

status.description: Filter executions by status (failed | succeeded | all)

name: faultmanager_get_health
description: Get health status of the Fault Manager service

name: faultmanager_get_alert_inventory
description: Get inventory of all possible alerts

name: faultmanager_get_alert_history
description: Get history of alerts generated by the system

name: faultmanager_get_alarm_inventory
description: Get inventory of all possible alarms

name: inventory_get_getting_started
description: This API will be called when very first time the user logs in

device_ips.description: REQUIRED. List of switch management IP addresses to query interfaces for. XCO returns 404 if omitted.

name: inventory_get_router_bgp
description: Get the BGP configuration and operational summary for a device — local AS, router-id, peer groups, neighbors (remote-AS, BFD), and per-VRF address-family state. Target the device by management IP (device_ip) or device_id. Use this to answer "show BGP summary" / BGP status for a switch.

name: inventory_get_value_for_key
description: Get a specific key vale pair based on key_name.

host_ips.description: REQUIRED. List of firmware host IPs to look up. XCO returns 400 if omitted.

name: inventory_get_device_details
description: Get device discovery interval time & device details

name: inventory_get_device_inventory_structure
description: This API will return the device inventory structure information based on the type

name: inventory_get_device_inventory_ports
description: This API will return the device port inventory information

name: faultmanager_get_alarm_history
description: Get history of alarms generated by the system

name: faultmanager_get_alarm_summary
description: Get summary of alarms grouped by severity

name: snmp_get_health
description: Get health status of the SNMP service

name: snmp_get_executions
description: Get list of SNMP execution records

limit.description: Limit the number of executions returned

status.description: Filter executions by status (failed | succeeded | all)

name: snmp_get_execution
description: Get detailed SNMP execution output by execution ID

policy.disabled_reason: Execution list endpoint is unavailable (404); individual execution lookup not supported in XCO 4.0.0 GA

name: tenant_get_health
description: Get health status of the Tenant service

name: tenant_get_tenants
description: Get all tenants configured in the Tenant service

name: tenant_get_tenant
description: Get tenant details by tenant name

name: tenant_get_portchannels
description: Get all port-channels configured for a tenant

tenant_name.description: Tenant to which this port-channel belongs

name: tenant_get_portchannel
description: Get detailed Portchannel configuration for a tenant

name: tenant_get_bgp_peer_operational
description: Get operational state of BGP peers for a tenant

tenant_name.description: Tenant to which this BGP peer belongs

vrf_name.description: Optional VRF name to filter BGP peers

name: tenant_get_bgp_peers
description: Get list of BGP peer configurations for a tenant

tenant_name.description: Tenant to which the BGP peers belong

name: tenant_get_bgp_peer
description: Get BGP peer configuration for a tenant

name: tenant_get_mirror_sessions
description: Get mirror service sessions for a tenant

name: tenant_get_endpoint_groups
description: List all endpoint groups (EPGs) belonging to a tenant. tenant_name is required.

tenant_name.description: Tenant name (required). Use tenant_get_tenants to list available tenant names.

name: rbac_validate_authorization
description: Validate authorization for one or more roles against a method and path

name: rbac_validate_tenant_authorization
description: Validate tenant authorization for one or more roles

name: rbac_get_authorized_tenants
description: Get list of tenants authorized for a dynamic role

name: rbac_get_role
description: Get specific role defined in EFA

name: rbac_get_roles
description: Get all roles defined in EFA

name: rbac_get_executions
description: Get list of previous RBAC executions

name: rbac_get_execution_detail
description: Get detailed output of a specific RBAC execution

name: licensing_get_health
description: Get health status of the licensing service

name: licensing_get_licenses
description: Show all licenses added to XCO

name: vcenter_get_esxi_details
description: Get ESXi host details from vCenter

name: vcenter_get_physical_links
description: Get physical NIC to switch links

name: hyperv_get_physical_links
description: Get physical NIC to switch links for Hyper-V

name: hyperv_get_virtual_links
description: Get virtual NIC to logical network links for Hyper-V

name: fabric_get_fabric_overview
description: Tier-2: Compact fabric overview (headline + optional errors/devices summary). Set include_raw=true to include raw Tier-1 payloads (large).

fabric_name.description: Optional: filter to a single fabric by name

include_health.description: If true, enrich output with fabric health data (via Tier-1).

include_errors.description: If true, include fabric errors data when available (via Tier-1).

include_devices.description: If true, include per-fabric devices_summary (counts by role/firmware/config state).

include_raw.description: If true, include raw Tier-1 payloads (summary_raw/health_raw/devices_raw). Output becomes large.

name: fabric_get_fabric_health_summary
description: Tier-2: fabric health summary (global + per-fabric). Combines fabrics-health, fabric-health, service health, and optional errors into an actionable summary with next steps.

name.description: Fabric name. If omitted, runs in global mode.

include_service_health.description: Include fabric service health (fabric_get_health / service health).

include_errors.description: Include errors sections using fabrics-errors and fabric-errors (with fallback).

expand_unhealthy.description: In global mode, expand first unhealthy fabrics into partial summaries.

include_raw.description: Include raw Tier-1 per-fabric health payload as health_raw.

name: fabric_get_fabric_health_timeline
description: Tier-2: fabric health timeline (per-fabric). Builds a chronological view by combining current fabric health, event history, and execution history. Correlation between eventhistories.execution_uuid and executions is BEST-EFFORT (IDs may not match); when exact join is not possible, the tool falls back to heuristics (time proximity + command/fabric-name hints). Returns an actionable grouped timeline with next steps.

device_ip.description: Optional device IP filter for event history (if supported by backend; backend may return empty payload).

include_global.description: Include global fabrics list (fabrics-health) and validate the fabric name exists.

include_service_health.description: Include fabric service health (fabric_get_health / service health).

include_health_headline.description: Include current fabric health headline (fabric-health), with fallback to global list.

include_executions.description: Include execution list. Note: executions endpoint may not support name filtering; tool fetches system-wide executions and filters locally when possible.

include_exec_details.description: Optionally fetch execution detail per execution id/uuid (best-effort, only if endpoint exists). Limited by max_exec_details.

since.description: Optional ISO timestamp (UTC). Locally filter events/executions with time >= since.

until.description: Optional ISO timestamp (UTC). Locally filter events/executions with time <= until.

window_hours.description: Convenience time window (hours). Used only if since/until are not provided (now - window_hours .. now).

limit_events.description: Backend limit for event history fetch (global fetch, then local filter to this fabric).

max_events.description: Maximum number of filtered events returned for this fabric after local filtering/sorting.

max_timeline_items.description: Maximum number of grouped execution timeline entries returned.

exec_limit.description: Backend limit for execution list fetch.

exec_status.description: Execution status selector. Use 'all' for broad history (matches your working call).

max_exec_details.description: Maximum execution detail records to fetch when include_exec_details=true.

include_raw.description: Include raw Tier-1 payloads as events_raw / executions_raw / health_raw when available.

name: fabric_get_fabric_validation_report
description: Tier-2: Pre-change readiness / audit report for a fabric. Correlates health, errors, locks, and validation endpoints into a PASS/WARN/FAIL verdict with next actions.

include_health.description: If true, include fabric health headline + device health counts.

include_errors.description: If true, include fabric errors (and fail verdict if any errors exist).

include_locks.description: If true, include service locks (warn verdict if any locks exist).

include_validate_fabric.description: If true, call fabric_validate_fabric and include its findings.

include_topology_validation.description: If true, call fabric_validate_physical_topology and include its findings.

include_raw.description: If true, include raw Tier-1 responses under payload.raw (large).

name: fabric_get_fabric_errors_summary
description: Tier-2: Fabric errors summary. Calls Tier-1 fabric_get_fabric_errors (and optionally fabric_get_fabrics_errors + fabric_get_fabric_health) and returns an actionable summary (counts, top error types, affected devices when present) with next steps. Set include_raw=true to include raw Tier-1 payloads.

include_health.description: If true, also fetch fabric health (fabric_get_fabric_health) to include device health counts for context.

include_global.description: If true, also fetch global fabrics errors (fabric_get_fabrics_errors) for context.

max_error_items.description: Maximum number of error items to return if the endpoint provides a list.

include_raw.description: If true, include raw Tier-1 payloads under raw.{...}. Output becomes large.

name: fabric_get_fabric_execution_last_failed
description: Tier-2: Find the most recent FAILED fabric execution for a given fabric name (best-effort match), and return a compact actionable summary. Uses Tier-1 fabric_get_execution_list and optionally fabric_get_execution_get.

limit.description: How many failed executions to request from Tier-1 list before local filtering.

include_detail.description: If true, fetch execution detail for the matched execution (Tier-1 fabric_get_execution_get).

include_raw.description: If true, include raw Tier-1 payloads (can be large).

name: fabric_get_fabric_execution_recent
description: Tier-2: recent fabric executions (per-fabric). Fetches execution list, correlates executions to a fabric name/id using best-effort heuristics, optionally fetches execution detail for the most recent matched executions, and returns an actionable summary + next steps.

limit.description: How many executions to request from Tier-1 execution list.

status.description: Status filter passed to execution list: failed | succeeded | all.

max_items.description: Maximum matched executions returned in the output list.

include_detail.description: If true, fetch execution detail for up to detail_limit matched executions.

detail_limit.description: Max number of matched executions to fetch detail for when include_detail=true.

include_raw.description: Include raw Tier-1 payloads under raw.

name: fabric_get_fabric_efa_command_list
description: Return the EFA command/script lines (from XCO runningConfig) correlated to a specific fabric name

max_items.description: Max matched command lines to return (default 200)

include_full_text.description: Include full extracted script text (default false)

include_raw.description: Include raw Tier-1 outputs (default false)

name: inventory_get_fabric_switches_summary
description: Tier-2: Summarize switches that belong to a specific fabric. Composite tool: validates fabric exists (fabric_get_fabrics), fetches switches from Inventory (inventory_getswitches) using fabric-id, and optionally enriches a limited number of switches with per-device inventory summary (inventory_switch_inventory_summary_info). Returns breakdowns (role/model/status) + compact switch list + next actions.

max_items.description: Max switches returned in signals.switches.items (after local truncation).

include_per_switch_summary.description: If true, fetch inventory summary-info for a limited number of switches (best-effort, requires device_id).

per_switch_limit.description: How many switches to enrich with inventory_switch_inventory_summary_info when include_per_switch_summary=true.

include_raw.description: Include raw Tier-1 responses under raw (debug).

name: inventory_get_software_version_mismatch
description: Tier-2: Scan switches per fabric and detect firmware/software version mismatches grouped by fabric/role/model/global. Uses fabric_get_fabrics + inventory_getswitches.

name: inventory_get_device_health_rollup
description: Tier-2: Roll up device health to explain which devices are driving fabric health (e.g., Red). Composite of monitor_get_health_inventory + fabric_get_fabrics_health + fabric_get_fabrics + inventory_getswitches.

fabric_name.description: Optional fabric name filter (e.g., DC). If omitted, scans all fabrics.

include_healthy.description: If true, includes green devices in lists (otherwise only unhealthy per min_severity).

health_resource.description: Resource string passed to monitor_get_health_inventory. If it returns empty/invalid, tool will try safe fallbacks.

name: inventory_switch_inventory_info
description: Get hardware inventory information for a single switch

device_id.description: Switch device id (from inventory_getswitches or fabric switch lists)

name: inventory_get_unreachable_devices
description: Tier-2: Show devices currently unreachable/down (best-effort last_seen/last_error) using inventory + faultmanager alarms.

fabric_name.description: Optional fabric name filter (e.g., DC). If omitted, scans all fabrics.

include_alarms.description: If true, include top alarm snippets per unreachable device.

alarm_limit.description: Max alarm snippets per device when include_alarms=true (1..20).

unreachable_only.description: If true, return only unreachable devices (default). If false, return all devices with reachability classification.

include_raw.description: If true, include tier-1 raw responses (debug).

name: tenant_get_service_epg_health_summary
description: Tier-2: Real-time, table-friendly health summary for a tenant's VRFs + Endpoint Groups (EPGs), enriched via per-object error endpoints and optional recent execution signals.

include_rows.description: If true, include per-EPG table rows.

include_vrf_summary.description: If true, include per-VRF summary rows.

include_recent_executions.description: If true, include recent tenant execution rollup (tenant_get_execution_list).

include_events.description: If true, fetch event history for the most recent FAILED execution (best-effort).

include_locks.description: If true, include lock counts for service/vrf/epg.

include_raw.description: If true, include tier-1 raw responses (debug).

name: tenant_get_service_epg_alarm_summary
description: Tier-2: Show tenant-scoped Service/EPG alarms/alerts with filters (severity/type/state) using faultmanager history + tenant EPG scoping.

tenant_name.description: Tenant name (required). If not found, tool returns suggested_tenants via tenant_get_tenants.

severity.description: Optional exact severity filter (alerts, and best-effort for alarms). Example: Critical.

severity_min.description: Optional minimum severity (Critical>Major>Minor>Warning>Info). Applies to alerts and best-effort alarms.

alarm_type.description: Optional alarm_type filter for alarms (passed to faultmanager_get_alarm_history).

resource_contains.description: Optional substring filter applied to resource/name/message (post-filter).

message_contains.description: Optional substring filter applied to message/description fields (post-filter).

epg_name_contains.description: Optional substring filter applied to EPG matching (limits to alarms that match this EPG).

include_raw.description: If true, include tier-1 raw responses (debug).

name: tenant_get_service_epg_event_logs
description: Event logs for a tenant/service scope (filters: date range, severity, fuzzy query). Tier-2 composite over tenant executions + event histories.

name: tenant_get_service_epg_historical_report_stub
description: Tier-2 (read-only, v1): Generate a simple historical health/alarm summary for a tenant's Service/EPG scope for the last 7/30 days. Use-cases: (1) Weekly tenant health pulse (7d) summary + top impacted resources. (2) Monthly ops snapshot (30d) with severity breakdown. (3) Escalation focus: only Major+/Critical+ using severity_min. (4) Keyword hunt across history using query (e.g., bgp/vxlan/certificate). (5) Tenant discovery: if tenant_name is wrong, returns suggested_tenants + next_actions. (6) Debug/evidence mode: include_raw=true captures tier-1 responses. Composite uses ONLY existing tier-1 tools: tenant_get_tenant (+tenant_get_tenants for suggestions), tenant_get_endpoint_groups, tenant_get_vrfs, faultmanager_get_alert_history (time-bounded), faultmanager_get_alarm_history (best-effort, time-bounded locally).

tenant_name.description: Tenant name (required). If not found, tool returns suggested_tenants via tenant_get_tenants.

window_days.description: How many days back to summarize (commonly 7 or 30).

severity.description: Optional exact severity filter (alerts, and best-effort for alarms). Example: Critical.

severity_min.description: Optional minimum severity (Critical>Major>Minor>Warning>Info). Applies to alerts and best-effort alarms.

query.description: Optional fuzzy substring filter across alert/alarm text (e.g., 'BGP', 'VXLAN').

allow_unscoped.description: If true, include system/global (unscoped) alerts/alarms when tenant-scoped matches are sparse.

include_raw.description: If true, include tier-1 raw responses (debug).

name: fault_get_active_alarms_top
description: Tier-2 (read-only, v1): Show top ACTIVE alarms by severity and the resources they attach to. Composite uses ONLY existing tier-1 tools: faultmanager_get_alarm_history (active/unacked/uncleared/closed filters) and optionally faultmanager_get_alarm_inventory (detail enrichment). Output includes totals, by_severity counts, and top alarm groups with top impacted resources and sample instances.

top_n.description: How many alarm groups to return (1..100).

max_records.description: Max active alarm records to process from tier-1 (1..5000).

query.description: Optional substring search across alarm text fields (e.g., 'certificate', 'bgp').

alarm_type.description: Optional alarm_type filter passed to faultmanager_get_alarm_history (and inventory enrichment).

resource.description: Optional exact resource filter passed to faultmanager_get_alarm_history.

resource_query.description: Optional substring filter applied locally to resource (useful when resource needs partial match).

include_inventory.description: If true, calls faultmanager_get_alarm_inventory(detail=true) to enrich alarms with catalog details.

include_samples.description: If true, include up to sample_per_group alarm instances per top group.

sample_per_group.description: How many sample instances to return per group (0..200). 0 = counts only.

include_raw.description: If true, include tier-1 raw responses (debug).

name: fault_get_alarm_details_with_context
description: Tier-2 (read-only): Explain an alarm (what it is, what it impacts, and related recent context). Composite uses ONLY existing tier-1 tools: faultmanager_get_alarm_history (instances + resource/severity), faultmanager_get_alarm_inventory(detail=true) (catalog explanation), faultmanager_get_alert_history (recent related alerts by resource + time window), monitor_get_health_detail(resource) (resource health), and optionally tenant_get_event_history_list(device_ip) when device_ip can be derived from resource.

resource.description: Exact resource string to scope to (optional).

active_only.description: If true, fetches active alarms (unacked=true, acked=false, cleared=false, closed=false).

window_hours.description: How far back to look for related alerts (and optional tenant events).

alert_limit.description: Max related alerts to fetch per selected resource.

top_resources.description: How many top resources to include context for.

include_alerts.description: If true, includes related alerts from faultmanager_get_alert_history.

include_health.description: If true, includes monitor_get_health_detail for selected resources.

include_tenant_events.description: If true, and device_ip can be derived from resource, includes tenant_get_event_history_list(device_ip).

include_raw.description: If true, includes tier-1 raw outputs for debugging.

name: fault_get_fabric_health_related_alerts
description: Tier-2 (read-only): Find alerts that indicate fabric health restored/degraded recently. Composite uses ONLY existing tier-1 tools: faultmanager_get_alert_history (filter by fabric resource + time window), fabric_get_fabrics (validate fabric_name + suggestions). Produces summary counts + curated alert list + signal classification (restored/degraded/other).

alert_limit.description: Tier-1 fetch limit sent to faultmanager_get_alert_history.

severity.description: Optional severity filter passed to Tier-1 (e.g. Critical, Major, Warning, Info).

include_other.description: If true, include alerts that are fabric-scoped but not classified as restored/degraded.

include_raw.description: If true, include tier-1 raw outputs for debugging.

name: notification_get_recent_events_filtered
description: Get recent execution-derived events across services and filter by severity/type/resource/query (Tier-2 composite).

sources.description: Which execution sources to query (uses existing Tier-1 execution tools).

status.description: Filter executions by status when supported (failed | succeeded | all).

last_n.description: Return the last N events after filtering.

limit_per_source.description: How many executions to pull per source before extracting events. Each unit makes one HTTP call to XCO, so keep low (3-5) for fast responses.

event_type.description: Substring match against normalized event type.

include_raw.description: Include tier1_raw payloads for troubleshooting.

name: notification_get_last_failed_delivery_or_errors
description: Tier-2: Find the most recent FAILED notification execution (notification pipeline issues). Uses Tier-1 notification_get_executions. If backend returns no failures, can optionally fallback to status=all and detect non-success locally.

window_hours.description: Local time filter over execution start/end time (best-effort). 168h=7d.

limit.description: How many executions to request from Tier-1 notification_get_executions before local filtering.

status.description: Tier-1 status filter: failed | succeeded | all.

max_items.description: Maximum number of recent failed executions returned under recent_failed.

fallback_detect_non_success.description: If Tier-1 status=failed returns none, optionally re-query status=all and detect non-success statuses locally.

include_raw.description: If true, include raw Tier-1 responses under tier1_raw (can be large).

name: monitor_get_platform_quick_status
description: Tier-2: Single view platform status (EFA + services + health). Optionally fetch health detail for problematic resources. Set include_raw=true for Tier-1 evidence (large).

include_services.description: If true, include service status summary (monitor_get_service_status).

include_health.description: If true, include platform health summary (monitor_get_health).

include_health_detail.description: If true, fetch monitor_get_health_detail for selected resources (see detail_only_on_problem/max_detail).

detail_only_on_problem.description: If true, only fetch health detail for resources considered unhealthy/degraded.

max_detail.description: Max number of resources to fetch health detail for.

include_raw.description: If true, include raw Tier-1 responses under tier1_raw (large).

name: system_get_ha_and_node_health_summary
description: Tier-2: Summarize HA redundancy + node health + storage signals, and correlate with HealthManager resources. Uses only existing Tier-1 tools: system_get_health_status, monitor_get_keep_alived_info, monitor_get_gluster_fs_info, monitor_get_k3s_nodes, monitor_get_health (+ optional inventory/detail).

name: system_get_certificates_expiring_soon
description: Show certificates expiring soon (bucketed 30/60/90 days, UI-style color/severity). Composite of monitor cert expiry + device cert expiry.

name: system_get_certificate_alarm_context
description: Any current alarms about certificate expiry and which cert is it? Composite of faultmanager alarms filtered to certificate/expiry + certificate expiry metadata (monitor + device).

name: inventory_get_switches_widget_table
description: UI-ready switch inventory table (for MCP client widget). Composite: inventory_getswitches + optional per-switch health/status enrichment tool. Returns flat rows with fields matching widget columns (IP, Status, Name, Model, Type, MAC, Location, Firmware, Added On, Fabric).

fabric_name.description: If provided, filters the resulting table to this fabric (when supported by underlying inventory tool).

fabric_all.description: If true, attempts to include devices across all fabrics (if supported by underlying tools).

device_ips.description: Optional: restrict to specific device management IPs.

device_ids.description: Optional: restrict to specific device IDs (preferred join key if available).

include_status.description: If true, enriches each row with Status via a secondary tool call (status_tool).

status_tool.description: Name of an existing tool that can return per-device health/status keyed by device_id or ip.

max_items.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

include_raw.description: If true, includes the raw inventory record per row (for debugging).

name: restconf_show_firmware_version
description: Read-only: Query an SLX switch directly via RESTCONF RPC to return OS/firmware version + uptime (bypasses XCO).

switch_ip.description: Management IP/FQDN of the target switch (required).

username.description: Optional override RESTCONF username (falls back to RESTCONF_USER env).

password.description: Optional override RESTCONF password (falls back to RESTCONF_PASS env).

verify_tls.description: If true, verify TLS cert (else -k / insecure). Defaults to RESTCONF_VERIFY_TLS env if set.

include_raw.description: If true, include raw RESTCONF RPC response for debugging.

name: restconf_get_interface_detail
description: Read-only: Query an SLX switch directly via RESTCONF RPC (get-interface-detail) to return rich port/interface statistics (RX/TX octets/packets/errors) and state. Optional interface_name filter.

switch_ip.description: Management IP/FQDN of the target switch (required).

username.description: Optional override RESTCONF username (falls back to RESTCONF_USERNAME/RESTCONF_USER env).

password.description: Optional override RESTCONF password (falls back to RESTCONF_PASSWORD/RESTCONF_PASS env).

verify_tls.description: If true, verify TLS cert (else insecure). Defaults to RESTCONF_VERIFY_TLS env if set.

max_items.description: Limit number of returned interfaces in items[].

include_raw.description: If true, include raw RESTCONF RPC response for debugging.

name: restconf_list_operations
description: Tier-2: List RESTCONF RPC operations exposed by a switch (useful for discovery; filterable).

name: restconf_get_lldp_neighbor_detail
description: Tier-2: Get LLDP neighbor details directly from a switch via RESTCONF RPC (brocade-lldp-ext:get-lldp-neighbor-detail).

interface_name.description: Optional: filter to a specific local interface (e.g. Ethernet 0/1).

name: restconf_get_port_statistics_summary
description: Tier-2: Summarize Ethernet port counters (octets/errors) across ports using RESTCONF RPC (get-interface-detail).

top_n.description: Top N ports by total octets (default 5).

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_media_detail
description: Tier-2: Show media/transceiver (SFP/QSFP) details via RESTCONF (vendor/serial/temp/RX/TX power when available).

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload.

name: restconf_get_arp_table
description: Tier-2: Show ARP table (IP→MAC→interface) via RESTCONF RPC (brocade-arp:get-arp).

switch_ip.description: Switch management IP/FQDN.  Pass a single string for single-switch probe (original response shape) OR an array of strings for parallel multi-switch fan-out (v2 response shape: meta.multi_switch=true, switch_level_data_by_ip, errors_by_ip; per-switch failures non-fatal).  Single-element list also returns the multi-switch shape so widgets can render consistently.

ip_filter.description: Optional substring match on IP address.

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_clock
description: Tier-2: Show device clock/time status via RESTCONF RPC (brocade-clock:show-clock).

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_vlan_brief
description: Tier-2: Show VLAN brief summary via RESTCONF RPC (brocade-interface-ext:get-vlan-brief).

name_filter.description: Optional substring match on VLAN name (case-insensitive).

port_filter.description: Optional substring match inside VLAN ports/membership text.

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_vrf_summary
description: Read-only: Query an SLX switch directly via RESTCONF data tree to list configured VRFs (bypasses XCO).

switch_ip.description: Management IP/FQDN of the target switch (required).

name_filter.description: Optional substring filter on VRF name.

max_items.description: Maximum number of VRF items to return.

username.description: Optional override RESTCONF username (falls back to RESTCONF_USERNAME env).

password.description: Optional override RESTCONF password (falls back to RESTCONF_PASSWORD env).

verify_tls.description: If true, verify TLS cert (else -k / insecure). Defaults to RESTCONF_VERIFY_TLS env if set.

timeout_seconds.description: RESTCONF request timeout seconds (defaults to RESTCONF_TIMEOUT_SECONDS env).

include_raw.description: If true, include raw RESTCONF response for debugging.

name: restconf_get_ip_interface
description: Tier-2: Show L3 IP addressing per interface (IPv4/IPv6) via RESTCONF data tree (brocade-interface:interface). Uses XML parsing fallback for SLX builds that return non-strict JSON.

include_ipv6.description: If true, attempt to include IPv6 addresses when present.

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_running_config
description: Tier-2: Retrieve a running configuration SNAPSHOT via SLX /rest/config/running (vendor XML) - the static device configuration TEXT, NOT live operational state. Returns top-level config sections + identity fields (hostname/chassis). Do NOT use this to check status: for BGP session status use restconf_get_bgp_summary or inventory_get_router_bgp; for interface status use restconf_get_interface_detail. WARNING: output may contain sensitive data (usernames, SNMP communities, AAA/TACACS/RADIUS keys, certificates) - use only when authorized to view the full configuration.

config_path.description: Optional subpath under /rest/config/running (example: 'interface', 'threshold-monitor'). If omitted, fetches the full running config root.

max_bytes.description: Max bytes of raw XML snippet to include when include_raw=true.

include_raw.description: Include raw XML snippet for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_system_maintenance_status
description: Tier-2: Get system maintenance / maintenance-mode status via RESTCONF RPC (brocade-system-maintenance:get-maint-mode-status).

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_system_maintenance_rate_monitoring
description: Tier-2: Get system maintenance rate monitoring status via RESTCONF RPC (brocade-system-maintenance:get-system-maintenance-rate-monitoring). Some builds return HTTP 204 when not configured.

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: restconf_get_interface_all
description: Tier-2: Get all interfaces (management, ethernet, port-channel) via RESTCONF GET (brocade-interface:interface?depth=unbounded). Returns name, type, shutdown status, description, IP addresses, and channel-group membership for each interface.

include_raw.description: Include raw RESTCONF payload for troubleshooting.

name: tenant_get_all_endpoint_groups
description: Tier-2: Discover all tenants and return every Endpoint Group (EPG) across the system, grouped by tenant, with aggregate counts.

name: inventory_list_device_ids
description: Tier-2 SAFE_READ composite — the PAIRED LIST for device-id-required inventory tools. Returns device_ids[] + devices[{device_id, ip, hostname}] in clean snake_case so a client can feed an id straight into device_id-required tools (e.g. inventory_switch_inventory_info) without hard-coding a cached sample. Composes over tier-1 inventory_getswitches.

name: tenant_list_ids
description: Tier-2 SAFE_READ composite — the PAIRED LIST for tenant-required tenant tools. Returns tenant_ids[] + tenants[{tenant_id, tenant_name}] in clean snake_case so a client never hard-codes a tenant name from a cached sample. Composes over tier-1 tenant_get_tenants.

name: fabric_get_fabric_names
description: Tier-2 SAFE_READ composite — lightweight fabric discovery: list fabric NAMES (and ids) in clean snake_case (fabric_name, fabric_id), ready to feed into ID-dependent tools like fabric_get_overlay_topology(fabric_name=...). Pairs with the heavier fabric_get_fabrics so a consumer does not have to hand-pick items[0].`fabric-name` (hyphenated). Composes over tier-1 fabric_get_fabrics.

include_raw.description: Include the raw fabric_get_fabrics tier-1 response.

name: restconf_get_bgp_summary
description: Tier-2 (read-only): LIVE BGP session status per SLX switch via SSH (show ip bgp summary / show bgp evpn summary). This is the tool to answer 'check BGP status for switch X'. Returns per-neighbor state (ESTAB/IDLE/CONNECT/ACTIVE), uptime, prefixes-accepted, remote-AS and address-family (IPv4-unicast + L2VPN-EVPN), plus a fabric-wide all_healthy roll-up and an _agent_translation_note. Query switches by IP (switch_ips, string or list), by NAME (switch_names, e.g. 'Spine-1' - auto-resolved to management IPs via inventory), OR a whole fabric (fabric_name auto-discovers members). Requires switch SSH access (RESTCONF_USERNAME/RESTCONF_PASSWORD).

switch_ips.description: Switch IPs to query (a single string is also accepted). OR use switch_names / fabric_name.

switch_names.description: Switch name(s) / chassis_name (e.g. 'Spine-1'; a single string is also accepted) - auto-resolved to management IPs via inventory_getswitches. Use this when the user refers to a switch by name.

fabric_name.description: Fabric name — auto-discovers all member switches (via fabric_get_devices).

name: firmware_check_storage
description: Tier-2 (read-only): firmware pre-flight storage check. SSHs to each switch and runs `dir` + `show support` to report total/free flash (MB), whether free space is sufficient for a firmware download (>= 2000 MB), and whether core/FFDC files are present. Read-only — makes no changes. Requires switch SSH access.

device_ips.description: Switch IPs to check (a single string is also accepted).

name: inventory_get_chassis_info_bulk
description: Tier-2 (read-only): fleet-wide chassis identity export from XCO — one row per switch with hostname, IP, site, OS version, SERIAL NUMBER and part number (the same data XCO's 'Download Inventory' button produces, via inventory-bulk-export). One XCO call covers the whole fleet. Optional device_ids to scope; auto-expands to all switches in the bound XCO site otherwise.

device_ids.description: Optional integer device IDs (from inventory_getswitches.id / inventory_list_device_ids). Omit to auto-expand to all switches.

max_devices.description: Safety cap on auto-expansion; if the site has more switches than this, returns 400 — pass an explicit device_ids list.

name: restconf_slx_get_mac_address_table
description: Tier-2 (read-only): MAC-address-table per SLX switch via the `show mac-address-table` CLI (SLX RESTCONF exposes only config, not learned MACs). Returns per entry: vlan, mac (normalized), type, state, interface. Optional filters: mac_filter, vlan_filter (exact int), interface_filter. switch_ip accepts a string (single-switch) OR a list (parallel fan-out; per-switch failures non-fatal: meta.multi_switch, switch_level_data_by_ip, errors_by_ip). Requires switch SSH access.

switch_ip.description: Management IP of the SLX switch. A single string (single-switch shape) OR a list (multi-switch fan-out shape: meta.multi_switch=true, switch_level_data_by_ip, errors_by_ip).

mac_filter.description: Optional MAC filter: full MAC in any form (0011.2233.4455, 00:11:.., 00-11-.., bare hex) for exact match, or a substring (case-insensitive).

vlan_filter.description: Optional exact VLAN ID filter (pushes down to `show mac-address-table vlan N`).

max_items.description: Max items after filtering. summary.truncated reflects whether the cap was hit.

include_raw.description: If true, include raw `show mac-address-table` output under raw.show_mac_address_table.
